Politics.headline: Deputy Leader of the Government in the House of Commons to hold news conference on affordability measures
Politics.detail: The Honourable Arielle Kayabaga, Deputy Leader of the Government in the House of Commons, will hold a news conference to discuss the Government of Canada's actions to make life more affordable for Canadians, including the temporary suspension of the federal fuel excise tax and the Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit. The conference will take place on April 24, 2026, at 11 a.m. ET in Windsor, Ontario.

Politics.headline: Politics Friday: Will Michele Tafoya's public profile transfer to politics?
Politics.detail: Veteran sports broadcaster Michele Tafoya is running for Minnesota's open U.S. Senate seat as a Republican. MPR News will discuss with Tafoya what led her to run and what it will take to win in November.

Politics.headline: Government Releases Annual Report On Economic Inclusion
Politics.detail: The Australian government has released its annual report on economic inclusion, highlighting increases to the JobSeeker Payment and Commonwealth Rent Assistance. Labor is also expanding Paid Parental Leave to 26 weeks, making it easier to see a GP, making medicines cheaper, and investing in more housing.

Politics.headline: TV interview with Minister Butler, Sunrise – 24 April 2026
Politics.detail: Health Minister Mark Butler addressed Australia's housing crisis, acknowledging the government is behind target for building 1.2 million homes in five years. He mentioned efforts to address over-regulation and infrastructure work to speed up construction, while Deputy Opposition Leader Jane Hume criticized the government's progress and migration policies.

Politics.headline: Lebanon truce extended; Trump says 'clock is ticking' for Iran to make deal
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday. Officials from Israel and Lebanon met in Washington to build on last week's agreement for a 10-day ceasefire.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war impact seeps ever deeper into global economy
Finance & Economy.detail: The global economy is facing tangible strains from the energy shock triggered by the Iran war, as factories grapple with soaring production costs and activity weakens even in services sectors, major surveys showed on Thursday.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks mostly fall, oil rises as US-Iran talks stall
Finance & Economy.detail: Global stocks mostly fell on Thursday, retreating after recent gains as investors tempered their enthusiasm for a quick end to the Middle East war, while the benchmark international oil contract, Brent North Sea crude, rose further above US$100 a barrel.

Finance & Economy.headline: US says forces boarded tanker carrying Iranian oil
Finance & Economy.detail: The US Department of Defense yesterday said its forces boarded a vessel in the Indian Ocean that was transporting oil from Iran, as disruption from a standoff with the Islamic republic continued to batter the world economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Govt reveals worst case oil shock economic scenarios for NZ
Finance & Economy.detail: Nicola Willis says NZ's recovery has been 'delayed, not derailed' and Moody's credit rating agency placed New Zealand's AAA rating on a "negative" outlook, warning that risks to the country's fiscal trajectory had increased, also pointing to rising government debt.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canadian and U.S. markets drift lower amid continued oil price volatility
Finance & Economy.detail: Canadian and U.S. markets drift lower amid continued oil price volatility. Meta slashes 8,000 jobs, or 10% of its workforce, as Microsoft offers buyouts.

Technology.headline: Samsung Workers Demand 15% Cut of Memory Profits, Threaten to Strike
Technology.detail: Samsung workers in South Korea are rallying and threatening to strike, demanding higher pay and a 15% cut of memory profits.

Technology.headline: Apple just fixed an iOS flaw exploited by the FBI - here's what happened
Technology.detail: Apple has patched an iOS flaw that was reportedly exploited by the FBI.

Technology.headline: Microsoft gives your Word documents an AI co-author you didn't ask for
Technology.detail: Microsoft is rolling out agentic Copilot in Excel and PowerPoint, letting 21st century Clippy lend a hand, and also gives your Word documents an AI co-author.

Technology.headline: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope trumps Trump cuts, is launch-ready ahead of schedule
Technology.detail: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, aiming for space after multiple near death experiences, is launch-ready ahead of schedule.

Technology.headline: Brute-force attack linked Rec Room user phone numbers to online identities
Technology.detail: A brute-force attack misused Rec Room's friend-finder feature to match phone numbers to the user names of hundreds of thousands of players, assembling a database connecting their online identities directly to their real-world contact information.

Science.headline: Scientists discover how the Twelve Apostles were formed - and their real age
Science.detail: Scientists at the University of Melbourne have uncovered how Australia's iconic Twelve Apostles were formed, finding tectonic plate movements over millions of years lifted and tilted the giant structures out of the sea, and that the Apostles are younger than previously understood.

Science.headline: Sweet discovery rewrites understanding of how our bodies store sugar
Science.detail: WEHI researchers have discovered that stored sugar (glycogen) can be directly regulated using ubiquitin – a critical protein, overturning 50 years of scientific knowledge and potentially paving the way for new treatments for diseases caused by abnormal sugar storage.

Science.headline: EXPERT REACTION: Major earthquakes stop with a jolt, causing whiplash for buildings
Science.detail: A new study of 12 major earthquakes shows that they stop fast, with a sharp backwards movement that can cause a whiplash effect for buildings, and this should be included in hazard assessments.

Science.headline: Forgotten Medicinal Plant Shows Promise in Fighting Dangerous Superbugs
Science.detail: A traditional medicinal plant, tormentil, shows promise against antibiotic-resistant bacteria in laboratory tests, suggesting its compounds could be useful in combating superbugs.

Science.headline: Millions Take These IBS Drugs, But a New Study Finds Serious Risks
Science.detail: A long-term study tracking over 650,000 Americans with irritable bowel syndrome is raising concerns about the serious risks associated with these drugs.

Health.headline: These 80-year-olds Have the Memory of 50-year-olds. Scientists Now Know Why
Health.detail: A study of adults over 80 with memory abilities comparable to people decades younger, known as SuperAgers, are rewriting what we thought was possible for the aging brain, as their brains either resist or withstand the damage typically linked to aging.

Health.headline: Omalizumab Plus Immunotherapy May Ease Allergic Asthma
Health.detail: A recent study suggests that omalizumab combined with immunotherapy could provide relief for individuals suffering from allergic asthma.

Health.headline: Regeneron Wins FDA Approval for First Gene Therapy for Genetic Hearing Loss
Health.detail: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved the first gene therapy developed by Regeneron for genetic hearing loss, marking a significant advancement in the treatment of this condition.

Health.headline: NIH Announces Meeting of National Library of Medicine Board of Scientific Counselors
Health.detail: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) has announced a hybrid meeting of the National Library of Medicine Board of Scientific Counselors, with some sessions open to the public.

Health.headline: Tiny fiber probe monitors three key biomarkers at once, offering faster patient insight
Health.detail: A newly developed tiny fiber probe is capable of simultaneously monitoring three key biomarkers, potentially leading to faster insights for patient care and medical research.

Sports.headline: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel to miss NFL Draft and seek counselling as photos expose six-year scandal
Sports.detail: New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel will miss Rounds 4-7 of the NFL draft and is seeking counseling after photos surfaced of him with a former reporter, Dianna Russini, from an incident in 2020 while he was coaching for the Tennessee Titans. The team has voiced their support for his decision to prioritize his family and well-being.

Sports.headline: 2026 NFL Draft grades: Does Fernando Mendoza Merit an 'A' for Raiders?
Sports.detail: Analyzing the draft grades and assessing whether Fernando Mendoza's selection by the Raiders merits an 'A'. The article provides an evaluation of the Raiders' draft pick and its potential impact.

Sports.headline: GWM Game Day Guide: They shall not be forgotten - Warriors
Sports.detail: The One New Zealand Warriors and the Dolphins will participate in an Anzac Round ceremony to honor those who have served, before their game at Hnry Stadium. A capacity crowd of over 34,000 is expected for the emotional tribute.

Sports.headline: NRL 2026: Transfer Whispers, Jake Averillo rejects St George Illawarra Dragons, Dolphins, contracts, future, Round 8
Sports.detail: Jake Averillo has rejected offers from the St George Illawarra Dragons and the Dolphins. Jamayne Isaako's move to the Melbourne Storm has created an opportunity for Averillo to remain with the Dolphins.

Sports.headline: Lee Hao-yu makes MLB debut for Detroit Tigers
Sports.detail: Taiwan's Lee Hao-yu went 0-for-3 in his MLB debut for the Detroit Tigers against the Boston Red Sox at Fenway Park, becoming the 19th Taiwan-born player to reach the big leagues. The Tigers ultimately lost 1-0 in 10 innings, ending their six-game winning streak.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland tells fans Zendaya 'looks amazing' as they step out ahead of her "The Drama' premiere in Paris amid marriage speculation.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z breaks his silence about being named in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Darrell Sheets Was Cyberbullied, Costar Says
Entertainment.detail: Reality star Darrell Sheets from 'Storage Wars' passed away, and reports have emerged that he was a victim of cyberbullying, according to a costar.

Entertainment.headline: TWICE reflect on a decade as a group: 'There's so much more we can do' | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: TWICE members Nayeon, Jeongyeon, Momo, Sana, Jihyo, Mina, Dahyun, Chaeyoung, and Tzuyu discuss their anniversary release, solo tracks, evolving definitions of success, and creative processes.

Crime & Justice.headline: 'Prolific' shoplifters caught with bin bags full of chocolate
Crime & Justice.detail: Two women in Auckland, aged 24 and 25, were arrested after police found them with rubbish bags full of stolen chocolate and other goods valued at over $1700.

Crime & Justice.headline: Exchange of gunfire inside Mall of Louisiana leaves 1 person dead and 5 wounded
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge occurred after an argument between two groups in the food court, resulting in one death, five injuries, and multiple suspects fleeing the scene; five individuals are in custody.

Crime & Justice.headline: US Soldier Charged With Using Classified Information To Profit From Prediction Market Bets
Crime & Justice.detail: A U.S. soldier has been charged with using classified government information for personal gain related to bets on a raid involving Venezuelan President Maduro, facing potential imprisonment for multiple offenses.

Crime & Justice.headline: ICE Arrests Pedophiles, Sexual Assailants, and Drug Traffickers During National Crime Victims Week
Crime & Justice.detail: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) arrested criminal illegal aliens convicted of crimes like aggravated sexual assault and drug trafficking during National Crime Victims Week.

Crime & Justice.headline: Teen charged with murder over Wainuiomata stabbing
Crime & Justice.detail: A 17-year-old has been charged with murder following the stabbing death of a man in Wainuiomata, New Zealand, earlier this month; the accused was initially charged with assault with a weapon.

Education.headline: Ateneo leads PH institutions in 2026 THE Asia Rankings
Education.detail: For the fourth straight year, Ateneo de Manila University leads Philippine institutions in the annual Times Higher Education (THE) Asia University Rankings, ranking in the 501-600 bracket. The THE Asia University Rankings measures higher educational institutions across the continent based on research, teaching, knowledge transfer, and international outlook.

Education.headline: MP Ginette Petitpas Taylor to announce funding for Parents for Bilingual Education New Brunswick
Education.detail: Ginette Petitpas Taylor, Member of Parliament, will announce funding for Parents for Bilingual Education New Brunswick on behalf of the Minister of Canadian Identity and Culture and Minister responsible for Official Languages on Friday, April 24, 2026. The announcement will take place in Moncton, New Brunswick.

Education.headline: CPS lunchroom workers' rally over better pay ends with two dozen police citations
Education.detail: Two dozen Chicago Public Schools' lunchroom workers and their supporters were cited for obstructing traffic during a rally to draw attention to protracted negotiations over pay and staffing levels. UNITE HERE Local 1, the union representing roughly 1,700 CPS lunchroom workers, planned the rally and civil disobedience outside district headquarters as negotiations over a new union contract have surpassed 10 months.

Education.headline: Staff fear 'death spiral' as Goldsmiths restructures again
Education.detail: Goldsmiths University is restructuring again, which is causing staff to fear a 'death spiral'.

Education.headline: New online tool seeks to correct biases in student evaluations
Education.detail: A new online tool has been launched with the goal of correcting biases that may exist in student evaluations.

Lifestyle.headline: How to idyll worship in Krabi
Lifestyle.detail: Vir Sanghvi explores the attractions of Krabi, Thailand, as a destination for relaxation and enjoyment of natural beauty, in the 'Rude Travel' lifestyle section.

Lifestyle.headline: I was always the first to message friends. When I stopped I lost my entire circle. Am I a crap person?
Lifestyle.detail: An advice column explores a situation where someone stopped initiating contact with friends and subsequently lost their social circle, questioning whether they are a bad person as a result.

Lifestyle.headline: You be the judge: my partner's hair cream is toxic for our pets. Should he give it up?
Lifestyle.detail: An article presents a dilemma where one partner's hair product is believed to be harmful to their pets, posing the question of whether they should discontinue using it.

Lifestyle.headline: Sweden’s secondhand clothing swaps offer a trendy way to cut environmental waste
Lifestyle.detail: Thousands of people have attended Sweden’s annual clothing swap events across the Scandinavian country, providing a stylish and eco-friendly way to update wardrobes.

Lifestyle.headline: Ralph Lauren Sparks Fresh Backlash Over Rs 44,800 Bandhani Skirt
Lifestyle.detail: Ralph Lauren is facing criticism for selling a Bandhani skirt for Rs 44,800, igniting discussions about cultural appropriation and pricing.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Taiwan wise to China's 'incentives'
Opinion/Editorial.detail: China announced “10 pro-Taiwan measures” on Sunday last week following Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's (鄭麗文) trip to China and meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平), however, the Mainland Affairs Council warned they are “su

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Every cent of wealth is legitimate – Sara
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Vice President Sara Duterte said that every cent of her wealth came from legitimate sources, refuting allegations during the House impeachment hearings that she amassed unexplained wealth.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL - Go ahead, talk
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Resigned House speaker Martin Romualdez has complained that he is being used as a “scapegoat” for others' corruption, after being barred from leaving the country and facing a possible asset freeze along with criminal investigation for alleged plunder.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Inflation won't stop at pump - Editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: While government estimates suggest fuel hikes are a minor technicality, the real danger lies in a squeezed middle class whose forced spending cuts could trigger a much broader economic slowdown.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Scrap the submarine daydream
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan's first indigenous submarine, the Hai Kun (海鯤, or Narwhal), completed its first submerged dive, reaching a depth of roughly 50m during trials in the waters off Kaohsiung. By March, it had managed a fifth dive, still well short of the deep-water and endurance tests required be.

World.headline: Trump says Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire for three weeks
World.detail: According to Trump, a meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors to the United States went 'very well' on Thursday, leading to an extension of the ceasefire that was set to expire on Monday.

World.headline: Iran fires on 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: Amidst a declared ceasefire, Iran has reportedly fired upon three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil supply. This action complicates efforts to resume talks regarding the U.S. and Iran.

World.headline: Pilot's selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea's air force
World.detail: South Korea's air force has determined that a pilot taking a selfie was the cause of a mid-air collision involving an F-15K fighter jet.

World.headline: Indian seafarer among crew of ship held by Iran in Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: An Indian seafarer is among the crew members of a ship that has been seized by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, adding to the tensions in the region.

World.headline: US church boosts off-season attendance through Whodunit event
World.detail: An US church is seeing increased attendance during off-season periods by hosting 'Whodunit' events, which seems to attract more people.

Zimbabwe.headline: Prudent policies pressing for Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is seeing positive effects from implementing prudent policies.

Zimbabwe.headline: Boko rosy on Zim-Bots ties
Zimbabwe.detail: Relations between Zimbabwe and Botswana are looking positive.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimra opens tax amnesty window
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (Zimra) has opened a tax amnesty window.

Zimbabwe.headline: Tobacco sales hit US$369m milestone
Zimbabwe.detail: Tobacco sales in Zimbabwe have reached a milestone of US$369 million.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police crack down on illegal explosives
Zimbabwe.detail: Police in Zimbabwe are cracking down on the use of illegal explosives.
